elements.sent-2411-002: In this essay, we investigate some relations between CRN and ME, and report on several pleasant surprises which we had simply by putting these two topics together.
elements.sent-2411-003: Firstly, we propose a definition of ME models as a subset of CRN models, as stated in Open Problem \eqr{o:MEm .}
elements.sent-2411-004: Secondly, we review a fundamental stability result for boundary points, known in ME as the \NGM\ (NGM) method \cite{Diek,Van,Van08 , since it replaces the investigation of the Jacobian by that of a matrix whose origins lie in probability (the theory of branching processes).}
elements.sent-2411-005: This important result seems to be little known outside ME; even in ME, it has not been made clear before that the method gets sometimes the right answer, even though the conditions of the NGM theorem \cite{Diek,Van,Van08 are not all \satd--see Section \ref{s:ShiFei .}}
elements.sent-2411-006: Thus, beyond the theorem, there is a heuristic approach, the validity conditions for which are not sufficiently understood, as emphasized in the title of Section \ref{s:ACR ).}

elements.sent-2411-090: The NGM method is based on the projection of the Jacobian on the subset of infection variables, and is justified by conditions which may be found in \cite{Diek,Van,Van08 .}
elements.sent-2411-091: These seem to be satisfied in all ME models with polynomial rates, but not in the ACR example of Section \ref{s:ShiFei .}

elements.sent-2411-093: Note the NGM theorem has been designed to cover ODE models with very general rate functions, but with no assumptions on the algebraic structure; polynomial rate functions, and particular algebraic structures which might increase its domain of applicability have not been investigated.
elements.sent-2411-094: Our NGM heuristic consists of the following steps: \BEN \im Write the infection equations (corresponding to variables which may converge to $0$) in the form \be{M}\vi'= M \vi\ee \im Split $M$ into part of ``new infections" $F$ containing all (or a subset of) the non-constant terms with positive sign \cite{AABJ , and the rest, denoted by $-V$, which \saty\ that the elements of the matrix $V^{-1}$ are all positive (these are related to ``expected dwell times", of associated CTMC models \cite{Hurtado,AAB ).}}
elements.sent-2411-095: Use the associated decomposition \be{FV \vi'= M=(F-V) \vi\ee to replace the study of the spectrum of Jacobian $M$ ( \wrt\ the infection variables) by that of the ``next generation matrix" defined by \be{K K=F.V^{-1 .\ee \im Determine the spectral radius of $K$, the so called \brn\ $R_0$.\EEN \beR The first step is suggested by the well-known fact that infection variables are fast compared to the others, so the non-infection variables may be taken as fixed and their equations ignored, asymptotically.}}}

elements.sent-2411-308: This essay is a continuation of recent efforts of ourselves and others to investigate relations between Chemical Reaction Networks (CRN) and mathematical Epidemiology(ME).
elements.sent-2411-309: In this work, we reinterpret the Next Generation Matrix (NGM) method — central in mathematical epidemiology — within a more general structural framework for CRNs, focusing on Jacobian splittings at boundary fixed points.
elements.sent-2411-310: We apply this to several CRNs with absolute concentration robustness (ACR) and illustrate symbolic bifurcation analysis using our Mathematica package ``EpidCRN.m".
elements.sent-2411-312: This class of models, at least in the examples we studied, is close qualitatively to ME models, in the sense that they have an unique boundary equilibrium and a unique strictly positive one, and the latter enters the positive domain and becomes \LAS\ (LAS) precisely when a condition similar to the famous $R_0 >1$ is satisfied, where $R_0$ is the basic reproduction number of ME.
elements.sent-2411-313: This phenomena has been known as \STP\ (STP) \cite{Shuai13 , in cases where the (LAS) holds actually as \GAS\ (GAS).}
